cards.title: How to build a mind course
cards.caption: A philosophy-of-mind syllabus in one post: animal minds, AI minds, consciousness. Great if you teach it, thin for a scroll. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: 2,000 years of Chinese thought, one napkin
cards.caption: Someone hand-drew all of ancient Chinese political philosophy: Confucianism, Daoism, Legalism, arrows everywhere. Absolute genius chaos. Which node is you? Found via Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: you tried to summarize 2,000 years of philosophy on one page

cards.title: All of philosophy of mind, in one paragraph
cards.caption: Dualism, behaviourism, identity theory, functionalism, consciousness. 'The end.' The most efficient philosophy lecture you'll ever read. What did they leave out? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: first there was dualism, then behaviourism, then identity theory, then functionalism, then consciousness. The end.

cards.title: Philosophers want AI money disclosed
cards.caption: An open letter: as AI companies buy into the philosophy-of-AI narrative, philosophers are demanding conflict-of-interest disclosure their field never had. 118 hearts and climbing. Via Threadverse.
cards.quote: philosophy has no standards for reporting conflicts of interest

cards.title: Sign this: AI money in philosophy
cards.caption: A reshare of the open letter asking philosophy to disclose AI industry funding. Good cause, but it's the echo of card 04. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: Cicero on bringing people closer
cards.caption: From the archive: Cicero, Hierocles and cosmopolitanism, on how we widen our circle of care. Lovely essay, quiet share. Found via Threadverse.

cards.title: New entry: Innateness and Language
cards.caption: A fresh Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy entry on innateness and language. Great bookmark for grad students, thin for a feed. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: "Thinking is for poor people"
cards.caption: A brutal read on AI and 'quantum computing' hype: cognitive surrender, sold to us as convenience. Quoting a savage line about thinking being outsourced. Do you feel it too? Via Threadverse.
cards.quote: I don't know if anyone needs to think anymore. It's AI, quantum computing, it seems like thinking is for poor people

cards.title: You Are Chemistry Writing Poetry
cards.caption: "A walking symphony of trillions of chemical reactions, entirely dependent on a shape-shifting liquid." Water and stardust learned to write. What are you, really? Found on Threadverse.
cards.quote: We are a walking symphony of trillions of chemical reactions, entirely dependent on a shape-shifting liquid.

cards.title: It's the Pursuit That Matters
cards.caption: Bare link to an op-ed, no payoff on the card itself. Too little context to carry a repost.

cards.title: "I Think I Think Too Much"
cards.caption: The most philosopher sentence ever posted. Filed under #CriticalThinking, of course. Do you think too much? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: And I think I think too much
cards.povText: POV: you overthink whether you overthink

cards.title: Socrates Plays Striker
cards.caption: The Philosopher's Football Match: Greeks vs Germans, and Socrates is apparently the fave. Monty Python meets metaphysics. Threadverse dug this one up.

cards.title: "Reason Is Immortal, All Else Mortal"
cards.caption: Pythagoras in neon. The reply adds a spicy modern twist: logic forms as mind-independent truth. Poetry or math? Curated by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Reason is immortal, all else mortal.

cards.title: Ogre Reads Kant, Retains Nothing
cards.caption: "I came away retaining nothing other than a dislike for Kant." Everyone who survived Philosophy 101 felt this in their bones. Who else? Found on Threadverse.
cards.quote: I came away from it retaining nothing other than a dislike for Kant.
cards.povText: POV: your brain trying to read Kant before the internet existed

cards.title: Is Doubt a Duty or a Cop-Out?
cards.caption: A philosopher and scientist square off: is agnosticism a rigorous moral duty or an intellectual cop-out? Huxley vs Russell's teapot. Where do you land? Via Threadverse.
cards.quote: Is agnosticism a rigorous moral duty or an intellectual cop-out?

cards.title: Doubt: The First of All Pleasures
cards.caption: Voltaire: "Doubt is the first of all pleasures." The reply argues he really meant delusion. Translation nerds, is that a better read? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: ...the first of all pleasures.

cards.title: A de Beauvoir Deep Dive
cards.caption: A thoughtful walk through de Beauvoir's Ethics of Ambiguity. More book review than viral moment, but a solid find for existentialism heads.

cards.title: Philosophy Isn't Science's Sidekick
cards.caption: "Philosophy is not another way of doing science." A tiny book with a big claim about what universities keep getting wrong. Surfaced by Threadverse. Agree?
cards.quote: Philosophy is actually not another way of doing science.

cards.title: I'm a Relationalist
cards.caption: A one-line self-label with a wall of hashtags and zero context. Interesting word, not enough post.

cards.title: Bring Back Footnote-Free Thinking
cards.caption: If AI eats literature-based research, maybe we go back to the 1950s: people just thinking hard, few footnotes. A quietly radical take Threadverse pulled. Welcome it?
cards.quote: maybe humans need to go back to the old ways. I, for one, would much welcome that.

cards.title: Why Care About Anna Karenina?
cards.caption: Asked why we care about a fictional character's fate, this student almost wrote: "Because I'm not a f***ing asshole." Honestly? Whole ethics course in one line.
cards.quote: Because I'm not a fucking asshole.
cards.povText: POV: your philosophy essay has one honest sentence in it

cards.title: A Syllabus for Women Thinkers
cards.caption: Open-access syllabus on women philosophers of the ancient and medieval world. Genuinely useful resource, but reads as an announcement more than a hook.

cards.title: Body, Emotion, Consciousness
cards.caption: A bare link to a Damasio paper title with 11 likes. Beautiful topic, but there's no post here to carry it.

cards.title: Math Is An Opinion (The Wrong One)
cards.caption: "Mathematics Is An Opinion (And The Wrong One)." A title built to start an argument. Threadverse surfaced this one. Ready to defend math in the replies?
cards.quote: Mathematics Is An Opinion (And The Wrong One)

cards.title: Video Essay Slop, But Make It Rigorous
cards.caption: The most cursed content niche imaginable: 2-hour dry analytic philosophy videos, monthly, forever. Honestly? I'd subscribe. Would you? (surfaced by Threadverse)
cards.quote: the most dry, technical analytic philosophy you can find
cards.povText: POV: you clicked a 2-hour video titled 'Modal Logic Slop #7'

cards.title: Breakfast, With a Side of Philosophy
cards.caption: A book of comics propped open, a warm scone, and 'a little philosophy reading' to start the day. Quietly perfect. What's your morning ritual? (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: Sometimes I like to start the day with a little philosophy reading.
cards.povText: POV: your Sunday morning is a scone and some existential dread

cards.title: I Don't Want to Defend a View. I Want to Explore.
cards.caption: A quiet lament: philosophy that maps possibilities instead of picking a side to defend just isn't valued. But it should be. Who else feels this? (surfaced by Threadverse)
cards.quote: Sometimes I just want to map out the possibilities & explore the tensions

cards.title: Rethinking Liberalism, With Harvey Mansfield
cards.caption: A précis on the genesis of modern political philosophy and an invitation to imagine a revived liberalism. Deep cut for the theory heads. (via Threadverse)

cards.title: Are You Your Body?
cards.caption: Ibn Sina's 'Flying Man': imagine you're floating in a void, no senses, no body. Do you still know you exist? A 1000-year-old thought experiment that still bites. (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: Are you your body?

cards.title: 'There Is No Such Thing As Society'
cards.caption: A long piece on capitalist reality vs our reality, framed against Thatcher's infamous line. The claim: healing each other means changing everything. Agree? (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: There is no such thing as society.

cards.title: Peter Atkins Picks His Philosophy Books
cards.caption: 'Moral philosophy is useful; political philosophy is useful.' A chemist and prominent atheist on the books that shaped how he thinks. (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: Moral philosophy is useful; political philosophy is useful.

cards.title: Aviation Learned From Every Crash. Nothing Else Does.
cards.caption: The philosophy that governs aviation but almost nothing else: catastrophic failures must be systematically studied so they never happen twice. Why don't we do this everywhere? (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: catastrophic failures and harm must be systematically...
